title: Raw pointer types lack NonNull distinction — nullability not tracked in types
description: Raw pointer types (*const T, *mut T) are nullable, but the type system does not distinguish nullable from non-null pointers. A function signature fn deref(p: *const T) gives callers no information about whether null is a valid input. Code using raw pointers must track nullability via comments or convention. This is especially important for FFI wrappers where the C API specifies non-null semantics. A NonNull[T] newtype wrapper should be provided in the standard library (analogous to Rust's NonNull<T>) so that functions that genuinely cannot accept null can express that in the type signature.
close_reason: Added NonNull[T] to §1.5 Pointer Types: new/new_unchecked/dangling constructors, covariance note, FFI example showing correct vs wrong declaration.
